competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: congress_mirror
competitors.rules: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: Robinhood
competitors.rules: Real Robinhood Agentic cash account (••••) running the funded algorithms (100% momentum_breakout) live: each run-robin reruns their signals on the latest bar and trades the real account — buy fresh breakouts, exit on the strategy's sell rule.
competitors.note: Real money — the actual Robinhood Agentic account (••••), not paper. Dollar/share figures are scaled ×100 to the $10,000 display notional like the rest of the board (the live account is ~$100). Allocation: 100% momentum_breakout. Change it in state/robin_alloc.json or just ask.

competitors.name: You
competitors.rules: Your real Robinhood account, rebased to the shared origin so it's comparable. Performance only — holdings and trades are deliberately not published.

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

criteria.label: ≥60 sessions
criteria.detail: About three months of forward trading — enough history that the record isn't a lucky week.

criteria.label: ≥20 decisions
criteria.detail: Enough trades that the outcome reflects the rule, not one or two flukes.

criteria.label: max DD better than −20%
criteria.detail: Never lost more than a fifth of the book from a peak — survivable risk.

criteria.label: beating S&P 500
criteria.detail: Positive excess return vs the buy-and-hold benchmark — otherwise SPY wins by doing nothing.

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.action: Hold everything at 7/19's targets — nothing today clears the bar for a rebalance. PayPal's board rejected the $60.50 Stripe/Advent bid as too low (wants $65-70) but framed it as a negotiating stance, not a walkaway; CVX/XOM extended their breakout as oil kept climbing on continued Iran strikes; the TSM/NFLX exits (queued 7/19) settle at today's open; GOOGL keeps drifting up into Wednesday's print; LLY dropped 2.8% on no identified company catalyst, in a broad-tape red session (Dow -307). None of that clears the bar for new action — reacting to one session of mostly-confirming, partly-noisy signals is the exact regime-chasing mistake flagged in the 7/19 reflection (dropping CVX on one soft-PPI day, only to reverse two days later).
analyst.sizing: 63% deployed across 8 names (BAC 10, REGN 10, CVX 10, META 8, LLY 8, PYPL 6, XOM 6, GOOGL 5), 37% cash — unchanged from 7/19; today's queued CVX/XOM/LLY buys and TSM/NFLX/META-trim sells settle at today's open and land the book at these weights without a fresh order

regime.label: Same regime as 7/19, one session further in: hawkish-Fed/hot-oil macro intact, semis staged a modest oversold bounce, PYPL's M&A story clarified without resolving
regime.note: Iran/US tensions are still escalating, not cooling — the US completed a ninth consecutive day of strikes overnight, and oil pushed further into the close (WTI $83.23, +0.9%; Brent $89.22, +1.3%) even as diplomatic signals flickered. The S&P fell a modest 0.19% to 7,443.28, Dow -307pts (-0.59%) on the oil-driven risk-off, Nasdaq roughly flat (-0.05%). Semiconductors staged a technical bounce off Friday's rout (SOX +1.5%, MU +2%) after ending last week down >20% from its June record — a relief bounce inside a still-broken chart, not a new signal; the book has no semis exposure to participate either way after Friday's TSM exit. PayPal's board formally rejected the $60.50/share bid as inadequate at a specially convened meeting, but framed it as pushing for a higher price rather than a final no — Cantor Fitzgerald pegs fair value near $70, a second analyst estimate lands at $65.20, and Stripe/Advent are reported still engaged. The stock barely moved on the news (56.56 → 56.84), which itself is informative: the market is pricing continued negotiation, not a dead deal.

analyst.thesis: What changed since 7/19, one session later: three developments, none of which move the target weights. First, PYPL: the board's rejection is the headline, but the substance is bullish for the arb, not bearish — a specially convened board meeting that comes back with 'too low, we want $65-70' (Cantor Fitzgerald's estimate) rather than a flat no signals the board sees standalone value above the bid and expects a raised offer, and the stock's muted reaction (+0.5% to 56.84) confirms the market reads it the same way. The position holds at 6% rather than adding, for two reasons: RSI was already elevated into the news, and PYPL now carries two overlapping binary events — the M&A outcome AND a 7/28 earnings print, roughly a week apart — which is exactly the kind of stacked-catalyst sizing the book should size DOWN into, not up. Second, energy: CVX (189.67, +1.2%) and XOM (148.40, +0.7%) both extended Friday's breakout as oil pushed further on a ninth straight day of Iran strikes — the regime call from 7/19 is playing out as read, and the queued limit buys (CVX $189.30, XOM $148.85) filled into the move rather than chasing it. No add today: 16% combined energy exposure is already a real sector bet: sizing up further on a SECOND green day is the same discipline error as sizing down on ONE soft-PPI day, just in the other direction. Third, the two names that moved against the book without a clean story: TSM bounced to 402.54 (+1.0%) as the semi complex staged a technical relief rally (SOX +1.5%) — the queued exit still settles today, because the underlying reasoning (sector-beta risk with no dated TSM catalyst until October) was never about a one-day read, and one green session in a stock that's still down double digits from its post-earnings high doesn't reverse that; chasing it back in on the bounce would be buying confirmation, the exact pattern this book keeps being punished for. LLY fell to 1146.40 (-2.8%) with no identified company-specific catalyst — no FDA news, no pricing news, no Novo Nordisk headline found — in a broad-tape red session (Dow -307). The GLP-1 duopoly thesis (Mounjaro +125% y/y, Zepbound +80% y/y per Q1) is unchanged, so this reads as tape noise or profit-taking after LLY's own +6% 20-session run, not a thesis break; the resting $1,190 limit buy filled favorably into the drop rather than at Friday's higher close. BAC (60.44, -1.4%) and REGN (672.34, -0.6%) both pulled back modestly off recent strength — ordinary give-back after BAC's fresh 52-wk high Friday, no news found for either. GOOGL (352.10, +1.5%) drifted further from its 346.77 entry into Wednesday's print — the contrarian-into-earnings setup is intact, if slightly less cheap than two sessions ago. META (645.86, roughly flat) holds unchanged. Net: every move today is either a continuation of an already-underwritten thesis (PYPL, CVX, XOM) or unexplained single-session noise (LLY, BAC, REGN) that doesn't rise to the level of a research signal — the disciplined move is to let the queued settlements land the book at 7/19's targets and hold, not manufacture a new decision to justify running the report.

evidence.point: PayPal board: 'formally rejected the $60.50-per-share joint offer from Stripe and Advent International at a specially convened meeting on July 20, 2026, pushing for a higher price as the board expects negotiation rather than a final refusal'; 'Cantor Fitzgerald analysts peg fair value at $70 a share, while asset manager Davis Park Management has calculated its own estimate at $65.20'; 'Stripe and Advent continue as the most credible potential acquirers and maintain efforts to negotiate'; PayPal reports Q2 earnings 2026-07-28
PYPL price action on the rejection: closed 56.56 (7/17) -> 56.84 (7/20), essentially flat (+0.5%) on the headline — session range 55.28-57.16
evidence.source: data/snapshot.json 2026-07-20 (aggregated from Robinhood minute bars + session fundamentals — day-interval feed was still lagging Friday's close as of 7:11pm ET)

evidence.point: Macro 7/20: 'The U.S. completed its ninth consecutive day of strikes on Iran overnight'; 'U.S. crude futures closed 0.9% higher at $83.23 a barrel, while international benchmark Brent settled up about 1.3% at $89.22'; S&P 500 'fell on Monday as oil prices advanced', closing -0.19% at 7,443.28; Dow -307.16pts (-0.59%) to 51,839.26

Semis technical bounce: 'Chipmakers tried to recover some of their steep losses from last week, with Micron Technology... climbing almost 2%'; 'an index of semiconductors up 1.5% after ending Friday down more than 20% from its late June closing record high'

CVX 185.93-190.34 range, closed 189.67 (+1.2% vs Friday's 187.38); XOM 146.25-149.09 range, closed 148.40 (+0.7% vs Friday's 147.36) — both cleared their queued limit buys (CVX $189.30, XOM $148.85) intraday
TSM closed 402.54 (+1.0% vs Friday's 398.37) inside the semis bounce — still -12.5% vs the book's original 460.19 entry; the queued exit order settles at today's open regardless

LLY closed 1146.40, session range 1144.61-1187.12, -2.8% vs Friday's 1179.11 close — confirmed against a live Robinhood quote (last_trade_price 1146.405 at 19:59:57Z, i.e. the 4:00pm ET close) after a web search initially surfaced a stale/cached price for the same session; no company-specific catalyst found in a targeted search (FDA, pricing, competitor news)
evidence.source: data/snapshot.json 2026-07-20 aggregated bars ; Robinhood get_equity_quotes 2026-07-20 ; web_search (no matching 7/20 LLY-specific news found)

risks: PYPL: Stripe/Advent walk instead of raising the bid after the rejection — the stock gives back the M&A premium toward the ~47 pre-bid level; standalone 10-11x earnings and the board's own $65-70 fair-value framing are the floor, but a walkaway would hit the same week as the 7/28 earnings print, compounding the move
GOOGL joins the sell-the-beat pattern 7/22 (ASML, TSM, NFLX all beat and sold this cycle) — sized at 5% specifically because the pattern is 3-for-3 against earnings bets

The semis relief bounce (SOX +1.5% today) extends and the TSM exit proves premature by more than one session — accepted risk: the position had no dated catalyst to underwrite a recovery against until TSM's October print
LLY's unexplained -2.8% is actually the start of a real story not yet surfaced in search (a same-day news lag) — worth re-checking before the next tick rather than assuming pure noise

Middle East de-escalates suddenly (nine days of strikes raises rather than lowers this probability each day it doesn't happen) and the energy sleeve's breakout reverses; the 3.8%/2.8% dividend yields on CVX/XOM cushion but don't prevent that

Prior book, realized P&L, alpha vs SPY and reflection

rationale.BAC: Hold 10, unchanged. [DATA] Pulled back to 60.44 (-1.4%) after Friday's fresh 52-wk high (62.115) — no BAC-specific news found, reads as ordinary give-back. [ASSUMED] The rate-lever/NII thesis into a hawkish Fed is unaffected by a one-session pullback.
rationale.REGN: Hold 10, unchanged. [DATA] 672.34 (-0.6%), still near its 20-day highs, 16.5x-ish valuation floor intact. [ASSUMED] No new information to act on.
rationale.CVX: Hold 10, unchanged. [DATA] Closed 189.67 (+1.2%), cleared the queued $189.30 limit buy; oil up again on a ninth day of Iran strikes (WTI $83.23). [ASSUMED] The breakout continues to validate the regime call, but 16% combined energy exposure with XOM is already a full-sized sector bet — adding on a second green day is the same undisciplined reflex as cutting on one bad day, just inverted.
rationale.META: Hold 8, unchanged. [DATA] 645.86, effectively flat (-0.02%). [ASSUMED] Still the second-cheapest mega-cap; the hold-through-~7/29-print decision waits on Wednesday's GOOGL result as originally planned.
rationale.LLY: Hold 8, unchanged. [DATA] Closed 1146.40 (-2.8%) on no identified company catalyst, confirmed against a live quote after a stale web result briefly suggested otherwise; the resting $1,190 limit buy fills favorably into the drop. [ASSUMED] GLP-1 duopoly fundamentals (Mounjaro +125% y/y, Zepbound +80% y/y) are unchanged; treating an unexplained single-day drop as noise rather than a new signal, while flagging it for a re-check next tick in case the news lags.
rationale.PYPL: Hold 6, unchanged — no add. [DATA] Board rejected the $60.50 bid as inadequate, publicly wants $65-70 (Cantor Fitzgerald: $70), framed as negotiation not refusal; stock barely moved (56.56->56.84). [ASSUMED] The rejection is incrementally bullish for the arb (signals board conviction the company is worth more) but stacks a second binary event (7/28 earnings) on top of the M&A outcome inside the same week — sizing stays flat rather than pressing a position that already carries two overlapping catalysts.
rationale.XOM: Hold 6, unchanged. [DATA] Closed 148.40 (+0.7%), cleared the queued $148.85 limit buy; same oil/Iran tailwind as CVX. [ASSUMED] Second leg of the energy sleeve, same sizing discipline as CVX — hold, don't chase the second green day.
rationale.GOOGL: Hold 5, unchanged. [DATA] Closed 352.10 (+1.5%), now further from the 346.77 entry ahead of Wednesday's after-close print (consensus $2.90 EPS, $116.9B revenue). [ASSUMED] Contrarian-into-earnings setup intact but slightly less cheap than two sessions ago; no reason to add before the print itself resolves the thesis.

charts.title: PYPL daily close through the board's rejection of the $60.50 bid (7/20)
LLY daily close — unexplained -2.8% session on 7/20, no catalyst identified

reflection.looking_back: The 7/19 report queued six orders around exiting the two spent-event tech positions (TSM, NFLX) and rotating into the energy sleeve (CVX 5->10, new XOM 6), plus a small META trim and an LLY add via a $1,190 limit — all settling at today's (7/20) open. One session later: energy extended (CVX +1.2%, XOM +0.7%), PYPL's board news landed without breaking the arb thesis, and the two tech exits produced a mixed grade.
worked: PYPL: the board's rejection of the $60.50 bid, read correctly as a negotiating stance rather than a break (Cantor Fitzgerald's $70 fair-value estimate and the stock's muted +0.5% reaction both confirm the market read it the same way) — the 7/19 hold call was right not to trim into the board meeting.

CVX/XOM rotation: both cleared their queued limit buys and extended the breakout as oil pushed further on continued Iran strikes — the 7/19 regime call (energy is where the tape is paying) held for a second straight session.

NFLX exit: no longer in the book to grade directly, but the thesis that drove the exit (H2 deceleration guide, no near-term catalyst) needed no defense today — nothing in the tape argued for re-entry.
missed: TSM exit: settling today at a worse price than it would have been held — the semi complex staged a technical bounce (SOX +1.5%, TSM +1.0% to 402.54) the very next session. The underlying reasoning (no dated TSM catalyst until October, sector-beta risk the book can't underwrite) is still sound and one green day doesn't validate re-entry, but it's a real, measurable cost of the discipline, not a free option.

LLY: -2.8% with no company-specific catalyst found anywhere in a targeted search — a reminder that the book's process (name a dated event, underwrite to it) has a blind spot for pure sector-rotation/tape-risk moves that hit positions with no story attached. Worth a closer re-check next tick rather than defaulting to 'noise.'
reflection.adjustment: Hold every target exactly at 7/19's weights rather than react to one session of mostly-confirming, partly-unexplained moves. The specific discipline being enforced: don't chase CVX/XOM further after a second green day (mirrors, in reverse, the 7/15 mistake of dropping CVX after one soft-PPI day), and don't re-enter TSM off one bounce session without a dated catalyst. The only thing carried forward as an open action item is re-checking LLY's news flow next tick in case today's drop had a cause that hasn't surfaced yet.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Real money — the Agentic cash account (••••), funded ~$100 on 2026-06-29, running the funded algorithms (100% momentum_breakout) via run-robin with a per-order confirm gate. Figures here are the real, unscaled account.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.
rules.congress_mirror: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2026-01-02–2026-07-20, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.

sources.name: Luck baseline
sources.detail: 200 seeded random 5-name equal-weight buy-and-hold portfolios from the same universe, same sessions — the shaded band on the chart is their 10th–90th percentile. A strategy that never leaves the band hasn't shown anything luck can't.

sources.name: Graduation bar
sources.detail: The standing rule before any strategy earns real money at scale: ≥60 forward sessions, ≥20 decisions, max drawdown better than −20%, and positive excess return vs S&P 500. Tracked live on this board.
